untranslatable_reason: Signal requires explicit implied volatility (IV) and break-even volatility calculations using Black-Scholes/Black model, delta-hedging mechanics, and premium-based loss thresholds. These are derivatives pricing constructs not available in the allowed indicator set (which contains only price-action and volatility proxies like hvol_20 and india_vix). The core logic—comparing realized vol to break-even IV and managing delta adjustments—cannot be faithfully represented without access to option Greeks, IV surfaces, and strike-specific premium data.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy: used hvol_20 (realized volatility proxy) > india_vix (implied volatility proxy) for entry, and reverse for exit. However, this is a significant semantic loss: (1) break-even IV is option-specific and premium-dependent, not a market-wide implied vol index; (2) delta adjustments require real-time Greeks not available; (3) mark-to-market loss thresholds depend on option moneyness and time decay, not general price action. Recommend implementing this signal within a dedicated options backtesting engine with access to option chains and pricing models.
